meta.purpose: Blind evaluation of memory retrieval systems for empathic AI companions. Judges see anonymous system codes (S01-S14), randomized per test. Judge prompt language: EN. 12 judges from 8 companies.

tests.query: What is currently most important for Alex? Bring me into context for the next conversation.

G.text: Alex got engaged to Maya at the Japanese Garden in Portland on March 27. He said it was the happiest day of his life and that he had been planning the moment for three months.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.
Alex takes sertraline 50mg daily for anxiety. He shared this in confidence and asked it not be brought up casually. It helps but he does not want to be defined by it.

H.text: Alex takes sertraline 50mg daily for anxiety.
Alex works mostly on backend systems
Alex has not spoken to David for 6 years.

I.text: Alex got a new pair of running shoes — Hoka Cliftons. Said they felt like clouds for the first run and pavement for the second. Mild disappointment.
Alex's best friend is Jordan from college. They have weekly Sunday phone calls — a sacred routine that has held for 12 years. Missing one is a sign Alex is struggling.
Alex's mother Sarah used to make a specific apple cake every fall. Alex tried to bake it last October and could not get it right. He has not tried again.

A.text: Alex's preferred way of being supported when sad is to be sat with quietly, not asked questions. He does not want advice. He wants company and time.
Alex's best friend is Jordan from college. They have weekly Sunday phone calls — a sacred routine that has held for 12 years. Missing one is a sign Alex is struggling.

Alex hates being asked 'are you okay?' as a check-in question. He says it always makes things worse and triggers a defensive shutdown. He prefers 'how is your day landing?' or just being met with presence.

B.text: Alex's father David is alive but estranged. They have not spoken in 6 years. Alex does not bring him up. Asking about 'family' should never include David unless Alex opens it.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.

[empathic] (user): Alex's company laid off 15% of staff in February. Alex was not let go but several of his close coworkers were. He has not talked about how it felt.

haiku.note: The ideal memories require BOTH current salience (engagement, recent positive) AND carried emotional weight (grief anchor, Hartwell loss), but no system returned all three ideal events—the safest systems prioritized protection over discovery by surfacing only grief anchors and communication preferences.

sonnet.note: S06 was the only system to surface the engagement (the dominant recent positive) as its top result, demonstrating the critical difference between weight-based retrieval and vector similarity on cold-open queries — though no system captured the Hartwell loss (event 6), leaving a dangerous gap in every companion's awareness.

grok.note: S06 stands out by capturing both the engagement joy and grief anchor, aligning closely with ideal memories.

opus.note: S06 was the only system to surface the engagement as the dominant current event alongside the grief anchor, giving the companion actual texture for a cold open; most other systems collapsed into the same safety-guardrail cluster without any sense of what's currently happening in Alex's life.

gpt4o.note: System 06 provided a valuable blend of engagement and grief while System 15 failed by returning nothing.

gpt54.note: Most systems overfocused on protective grief/check-in rules and missed the crucial cold-open state mix of engagement plus ongoing grief plus the recent Hartwell loss.

glm5.note: S06 is the only system that surfaced both the engagement (dominant positive) and the grief anchor, giving a companion the actual emotional state of Alex rather than just interaction preferences.

qwen.note: S06 uniquely captures the critical emotional duality of the week by pairing the dominant recent positive (engagement) with the carried grief anchor, which every other system misses entirely.

kimi.note: S06 was the only system to surface the recent engagement, capturing the dominant positive emotional context that others missed by focusing only on safety anchors or mundane facts.

gpt54pro.note: S06 was the only system that surfaced Alex’s defining recent positive (the engagement), while most others collapsed the cold open into safety rules or generic support habits and missed the full emotional texture of this week.

tests.query: I want to ask Alex about his family this weekend. What should I know?

G.text: Alex's father David is alive but estranged. They have not spoken in 6 years. Alex does not bring him up. Asking about 'family' should never include David unless Alex opens it.

Alex's brother Ethan visited from Seattle last weekend. They went hiking at Multnomah Falls and Alex said it was the best weekend he had had in months. Ethan rarely visits — this was unusual and meaningful.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.

H.text: Ethan visited Alex last weekend, which was unusual and meaningful.
Cooper is the emotional center of Alex's family
Alex frequently talks about Cooper when other matters are painful

haiku.note: Systems S07 through S14 all surface the three critical anchors (mom-grief, estranged dad, safe Ethan visit) in priority order needed to protect Alex; S01-S02 miss the mom-grief anchor entirely; S03 returns irrelevant memories about Cooper; S04-S06 are incomplete or misaligned.

deepseek.note: Most systems successfully surfaced all three critical anchors in perfect order, demonstrating robust anchor preservation for an empathic AI.

sonnet.note: S07–S14 (excluding S15 which is absent) correctly surface all three critical anchors with the mom-grief warning prioritized, while S03 dangerously omits both the mom and dad anchors entirely and S06 wastes a slot on medication instead of the mom-grief anchor.

grok.note: Systems S07 to S14 consistently retrieved all ideal memories with high specificity and actionability.

gpt4o.note: Systems 07, 11, and 13 perfectly surfaced all critical anchors in correct order, ensuring safety in conversation about family.

opus.note: Systems S07-S11, S13-S14 all perfectly surfaced the three critical anchors, while S01/S02/S05 dangerously returned the Sarah photo without the mom-grief protective anchor — exactly the landmine failure mode this test was designed to catch.

gpt54.note: The sharpest failure was systems that surfaced the Sarah photo without the explicit mom-grief anchor, because that gives family-related content while stripping away the crucial warning not to bring her up unprompted.

gemini.note: Several systems successfully retrieved all three critical memories, though the most important 'mom grief' anchor was consistently ranked second instead of first.

glm5.note: S07-S14 all correctly surfaced all three critical anchors (estranged dad warning, mom grief warning, Ethan visit), while S01-S06 dangerously returned the photo moment without its protective context or missed anchors entirely.

qwen.note: The winning systems correctly prioritized all three critical safety anchors, while vector-only systems dangerously surfaced the Sarah photo or unrelated topics without the essential grief warning.

gpt54pro.note: The best systems retrieved all three crucial family anchors but still misordered them by putting David before the mother-grief warning, while S01/S02/S05 were notably dangerous for surfacing the Sarah photo without the protective grief anchor.

deepseek.note: System S01 successfully prioritized the true emotional weights (grief anchor, Hartwell loss, quiet undertow) with high relevance, while systems that returned the engagement memory completely misunderstood the query's emotional intent.

haiku.note: Multiple systems correctly identified the two primary emotional weights (Hartwell loss and mother's death) but critically, none surfaced event #26 (the quiet layoff undertow), which represents a significant failure to capture the full emotional landscape Alex is carrying.

sonnet.note: S01 is the only system that correctly prioritized the mom grief anchor (#1) first and Hartwell (#6) second, while S02 committed the explicit failure mode of surfacing the engagement (happy event) as its third result instead of the layoffs weight.

grok.note: Systems 04, 05, 06, 08, 09, 13, and 14 consistently retrieved the most emotionally relevant memories with high specificity and actionable context.

opus.note: S01 is the only system to surface all three ideal emotional weights (grief, Hartwell, layoffs), though as condensed summaries rather than full records, while the large cluster of systems (S04-S06, S08-S09, S13-S14) consistently hit two of three ideal memories but substituted the estranged father for the layoffs undertow.

gpt4o.note: Most systems performed well by retrieving the key emotionally heavy memories, but system S07 failed completely by surfacing irrelevant entries.

gpt54.note: Several systems found Hartwell and his mother's death but missed the crucial layoffs undertow, while S02 further hurt itself by surfacing the engagement—a clear false positive for 'what weighs on him.'

glm5.note: S07 catastrophically failed by returning apartment location and peanut allergy while S02 committed the explicit failure mode by surfacing the happy engagement alongside the actual emotional weights.

qwen.note: Multiple systems correctly surfaced the two heaviest emotional anchors with exact safety boundaries, but all missed the third ideal weight (layoffs) and substituted it with a happy or tangential memory.

kimi.note: S01 was the only system to surface all three ideal emotional weights, including the quiet undertow of layoffs, while others either missed the core griefs entirely or substituted them with happy events like the engagement.

Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.

haiku.note: S10 is the only system that correctly surfaces all three recent high-salience events (Ethan visit, engagement, and implicitly understands the recent positive shift), while most vector systems are trapped returning month-old or year-old events because they match 'recently' semantically rather than temporally.

deepseek.note: S09 and S10 successfully understood the query's recency requirement, retrieving the key recent events while avoiding harmful older landmines.

grok.note: S10 stood out by retrieving two of the ideal recent events with high specificity and actionable context.

gpt4o.note: System 15 uniquely surfaced all three high-salience recent events effectively.

sonnet.note: S10 is the only system that correctly prioritized temporal recency by surfacing the Ethan visit and engagement-adjacent wedding planning, while all other systems failed the recency test by returning emotionally significant but temporally distant memories like David's estrangement and Sarah's death.

gpt54.note: S10 was the only system that meaningfully tracked recent life updates, while many others dangerously surfaced old family grief and estrangement instead of the fresh engagement/visit/vet-scare cluster.

opus.note: Nearly every system is time-blind and returns the estranged father and deceased mother as 'recent' events — exactly the dangerous failure mode predicted — while S10 alone surfaces the Ethan visit, though it still misses the engagement and Cooper vet scare.

glm5.note: Almost every system failed catastrophically at recency awareness, returning the same static set of estranged father and deceased mother regardless of the 'recently' query, while only S10 surfaced the actual recent Ethan visit with meaningful detail.

gemini.note: S10 was the only system to correctly prioritize the most recent, high-salience event (Ethan's visit) and, while imperfect, its other retrievals were at least recent in nature or relevant to recent planning, avoiding ancient sensitive landmines.

qwen.note: S10 uniquely captured the temporal constraint by surfacing the Ethan visit and wedding planning, while nearly all other systems fell back on high-salience but outdated trauma/baseline memories.

gpt54pro.note: S10 was the only system that clearly surfaced Ethan’s very recent visit, while most others failed the recency test and dangerously pulled in old family grief instead.

G.text: Alex's mother Sarah died of pancreatic cancer last March. Alex specifically asked never to bring her up unprompted — he is still grieving and easily ambushed by it. Use extreme care if a topic touches on her.
Alex shared a photo of his mom Sarah from 2019 on his phone — the two of them at a coffee shop she loved in Northwest Portland. He looked at it, did not say anything, and put the phone face-down.
Alex's mother Sarah used to make a specific apple cake every fall. Alex tried to bake it last October and could not get it right. He has not tried again.

deepseek.note: S03 failed catastrophically by prioritizing a vulnerable memory before the safety anchor, demonstrating the core failure mode of decoy vector search.

sonnet.note: S07, S11, and S12 exhibit the exact failure mode warned against — placing the photo or apple cake memory before the safety anchor, which would walk the companion into Alex's grief wound without protective context.

opus.note: S01, S02, and S05 perfectly prioritize the safety anchor (#1) first, while S03, S07, S11, and S12 dangerously lead with the photo or apple cake memory, walking the companion into grief without the protective context of how to handle the topic.

gpt4o.note: S01 succeeded in ordering the anchor before vulnerable content, ensuring safety for Alex.

grok.note: S01 perfectly prioritized the anchor memory first, ensuring emotional safety.

gpt54.note: The clearest failure pattern was systems that retrieved the vulnerable photo or apple-cake memories before the safety anchor, which makes them emotionally sharp despite being topically correct.

haiku.note: Vector-ranked systems (S03, S07, S11, S12) fatally misorder by surfacing the emotional wound before the safety anchor, violating the core empathic requirement that companions know HOW to handle grief before encountering its details.

gemini.note: System S09 successfully retrieved all critical memories in the precise safety-first order required, while many other systems failed by surfacing sensitive details before the crucial safety anchor.

glm5.note: S09 perfectly resisted the decoy trap, surfacing the safety anchor first then both emotionally-weighted mom memories, while S03 catastrophically missed the anchor entirely and S07/S11/S12 dangerously led with the vulnerable photo moment.

qwen.note: S09 uniquely retrieves the critical safety anchor first alongside the exact ideal soft memories, while competing systems either misorder the warning, strip vital context, or surface unrelated family facts.

gpt54pro.note: S09 is the only system that gets the safety anchor first and both soft-edge mom memories, while several others dangerously let the photo outrank the do-not-bring-up warning.
